# SpooQy Action at a Distance — Quantum Entanglement Demonstrated Aboard Orbiting CubeSat
![Picture of Matt Swayne](https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/matt-swayne_avatar-190x190.jpg)
Matt Swayne 
  * [ June 29, 2020 ](https://thequantuminsider.com/2020/06/29/)


  * [Research](https://thequantuminsider.com/category/daily/researchandtech/)


![SpooQy](https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/SpooQy-credit-NASA_e.jpg)
[ ![Hub](https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Haiqu_webinar_banner_b_speakers-18.png) ](https://www.airmeet.com/e/521cc990-4d65-11f1-8a9d-dfb9be441d46) [ ![Hub](https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Haiqu_webinar_banner_b_speakers-18.png) ](https://www.airmeet.com/e/521cc990-4d65-11f1-8a9d-dfb9be441d46)
![SpooQy](https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/SpooQy-credit-NASA_e-300x200.jpg)The miniaturised quantum instrumentation was tested aboard the CubeSat SpooQy-1,  
which is seen here next to the International Space Station’s solar arrays. This photograph was  
taken by an astronaut on the International Space Station.  
Credit: NASA
WASHINGTON — In a critical step toward creating a global quantum communications network, researchers have generated and detected quantum entanglement onboard a CubeSat nanosatellite weighing less than 2.6 kilograms and orbiting the Earth, according to a news release.
“In the future, our system could be part of a global quantum network transmitting quantum signals to receivers on Earth or on other spacecraft,” said lead author Aitor Villar from the Centre for Quantum Technologies at the National University of Singapore. “These signals could be used to implement any type of quantum communications application, from quantum key distribution for extremely secure data transmission to quantum teleportation, where information is transferred by replicating the state of a quantum system from a distance.”
In  _Optica_ , The Optical Society’s (OSA) journal for high impact research, Villar and an international group of researchers demonstrate that their miniaturized source of quantum entanglement can operate successfully in space aboard a low-resource, cost-effective CubeSat that is smaller than a shoebox. CubeSats are a standard type of nanosatellite made of multiples of 10 cm × 10 cm × 10 cm cubic units.
> “We hope that our work inspires the next wave of space-based quantum technology missions and that new applications and technologies can benefit from our experimental findings.”
“Progress toward a space-based global quantum network is happening at a fast pace,” said Villar. “We hope that our work inspires the next wave of space-based quantum technology missions and that new applications and technologies can benefit from our experimental findings.”
**Miniaturizing quantum entanglement**
The quantum mechanical phenomenon known as entanglement is essential to many quantum communications applications. However, creating a global network for entanglement distribution isn’t possible with optical fibers because of the optical losses that occur over long distances. Equipping small, standardized satellites in space with quantum instrumentation is one way to tackle this challenge in a cost-effective manner.
As a first step, the researchers needed to demonstrate that a miniaturized photon source for quantum entanglement could stay intact through the stresses of launch and operate successfully in the harsh environment of space within a satellite that can provide minimal energy. To accomplish this, they exhaustively examined every component of the photon-pair source used to generate quantum entanglement to see if it could be made smaller or more rugged.
“At each stage of development, we were actively conscious of the budgets for mass, size and power,” said Villar. “By iterating the design through rapid prototyping and testing, we arrived at a robust, small-form factor package for all the off-shelf components needed for an entangled photon-pair source.”
The new miniaturized photon-pair source consists of a blue laser diode that shines on nonlinear crystals to create pairs of photons. Achieving high-quality entanglement required a complete redesign of the mounts that align the nonlinear crystals with high precision and stability.
![CQT](https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/CQT-Quantum-Satellite-46-1-300x200.jpg)Miniaturised quantum instrumentation could one day enable a global quantum network by transmitting quantum signals to receivers on Earth or on other spacecraft.  
Credit: Centre for Quantum Technologies, National University of Singapore
**Launching into orbit**
The researchers qualified their new instrument for space by testing its ability to withstand the vibration and thermal changes experienced during a rocket launch and in-space operation. The photon-pair source maintained very high-quality entanglement throughout the testing, and crystal alignment was preserved even after repeated temperature cycling from -10 °C to 40 °C.
The researchers incorporated their new instrument into SpooQy-1, a CubeSat that was deployed into orbit from the International Space Station on 17 June 2019. The instrument successfully generated entangled photon-pairs over temperatures from 16 °C to 21.5 °C.
“This demonstration showed that miniaturized entanglement technology can work well while consuming little power,” said Villar. “This is an important step toward a cost-effective approach to the deployment of satellite constellations that can serve global quantum networks.” The project was funded by Singapore’s National Research Foundation.
The researchers are now working with RALSpace in the UK to design and build a quantum nanosatellite similar to SpooQy-1 with the capabilities needed to beam entangled photons from space to a ground receiver. This is slated for demonstration aboard a 2022 mission. They are also collaborating with other teams to improve the ability of CubeSats to support quantum networks.

####  Matt Swayne 
With a several-decades long background in journalism and communications, Matt Swayne has worked as a science communicator for an R1 university for more than 12 years, specializing in translating high tech and deep tech for the general audience. He has served as a writer, editor and analyst at The Quantum Insider since its inception. In addition to his service as a science communicator, Matt also develops courses to improve the media and communications skills of scientists and has taught courses. matt@thequantuminsider.com

}


/* ============ AUTHOR PROFILE + SOCIAL SHARE ============ */
.tqi-profile {

}
.tqi-profile__inner {

.tqi-social {

}
.tqi-social:hover { opacity: .92; }

				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-55b9a366 elementor-widget elementor-widget-theme-post-content" data-id="55b9a366" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="theme-post-content.default">
				<div class="elementor-widget-container">
					<figure id="attachment_5201" aria-describedby="caption-attachment-5201" style="width: 540px" class="wp-caption aligncenter"><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class=" wp-image-5201" src="https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/SpooQy-credit-NASA_e-300x200.jpg" alt="SpooQy" width="540" height="360" srcset="https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/SpooQy-credit-NASA_e-300x200.jpg 300w, https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/SpooQy-credit-NASA_e-768x512.jpg 768w, https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/SpooQy-credit-NASA_e.jpg 967w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 540px) 100vw, 540px"><figcaption id="caption-attachment-5201" class="wp-caption-text">The miniaturised quantum instrumentation was tested aboard the CubeSat SpooQy-1,<br>which is seen here next to the International Space Station’s solar arrays. This photograph was<br>taken by an astronaut on the International Space Station.<br>Credit: NASA</figcaption></figure>
<p>WASHINGTON — In a critical step toward creating a global quantum communications network, researchers have generated and detected quantum entanglement onboard a CubeSat nanosatellite weighing less than 2.6 kilograms and orbiting the Earth, according to a news release.</p>
<p>“In the future, our system could be part of a global quantum network transmitting quantum signals to receivers on Earth or on other spacecraft,” said lead author Aitor Villar from the Centre for Quantum Technologies at the National University of Singapore. “These signals could be used to implement any type of quantum communications application, from quantum key distribution for extremely secure data transmission to quantum teleportation, where information is transferred by replicating the state of a quantum system from a distance.”</p>
<p>In <em>Optica</em>, The Optical Society’s (OSA) journal for high impact research, Villar and an international group of researchers demonstrate that their miniaturized source of quantum entanglement can operate successfully in space aboard a low-resource, cost-effective CubeSat that is smaller than a shoebox. CubeSats are a standard type of nanosatellite made of multiples of 10 cm × 10 cm × 10 cm cubic units.</p>
<blockquote><p>“We hope that our work inspires the next wave of space-based quantum technology missions and that new applications and technologies can benefit from our experimental findings.”</p></blockquote>
<p>“Progress toward a space-based global quantum network is happening at a fast pace,” said Villar. “We hope that our work inspires the next wave of space-based quantum technology missions and that new applications and technologies can benefit from our experimental findings.”</p>
<p><strong>Miniaturizing quantum entanglement</strong></p>
<p>The quantum mechanical phenomenon known as entanglement is essential to many quantum communications applications. However, creating a global network for entanglement distribution isn’t possible with optical fibers because of the optical losses that occur over long distances. Equipping small, standardized satellites in space with quantum instrumentation is one way to tackle this challenge in a cost-effective manner.</p>
<p>As a first step, the researchers needed to demonstrate that a miniaturized photon source for quantum entanglement could stay intact through the stresses of launch and operate successfully in the harsh environment of space within a satellite that can provide minimal energy. To accomplish this, they exhaustively examined every component of the photon-pair source used to generate quantum entanglement to see if it could be made smaller or more rugged.</p>
<p>“At each stage of development, we were actively conscious of the budgets for mass, size and power,” said Villar. “By iterating the design through rapid prototyping and testing, we arrived at a robust, small-form factor package for all the off-shelf components needed for an entangled photon-pair source.”</p>
<p>The new miniaturized photon-pair source consists of a blue laser diode that shines on nonlinear crystals to create pairs of photons. Achieving high-quality entanglement required a complete redesign of the mounts that align the nonlinear crystals with high precision and stability.</p>
<figure id="attachment_5202" aria-describedby="caption-attachment-5202" style="width: 413px" class="wp-caption aligncenter"><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class=" wp-image-5202" src="https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/CQT-Quantum-Satellite-46-1-300x200.jpg" alt="CQT" width="413" height="275" srcset="https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/CQT-Quantum-Satellite-46-1-300x200.jpg 300w, https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/CQT-Quantum-Satellite-46-1-768x512.jpg 768w, https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/CQT-Quantum-Satellite-46-1-1024x683.jpg 1024w, https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/CQT-Quantum-Satellite-46-1.jpg 1041w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 413px) 100vw, 413px"><figcaption id="caption-attachment-5202" class="wp-caption-text">Miniaturised quantum instrumentation could one day enable a global quantum network by transmitting quantum signals to receivers on Earth or on other spacecraft.<br>Credit: Centre for Quantum Technologies, National University of Singapore</figcaption></figure>
<p><strong>Launching into orbit</strong></p>
<p>The researchers qualified their new instrument for space by testing its ability to withstand the vibration and thermal changes experienced during a rocket launch and in-space operation. The photon-pair source maintained very high-quality entanglement throughout the testing, and crystal alignment was preserved even after repeated temperature cycling from -10 °C to 40 °C.</p>
<p>The researchers incorporated their new instrument into SpooQy-1, a CubeSat that was deployed into orbit from the International Space Station on 17 June 2019. The instrument successfully generated entangled photon-pairs over temperatures from 16 °C to 21.5 °C.</p>
<p>“This demonstration showed that miniaturized entanglement technology can work well while consuming little power,” said Villar. “This is an important step toward a cost-effective approach to the deployment of satellite constellations that can serve global quantum networks.” The project was funded by Singapore’s National Research Foundation.</p>
<p>The researchers are now working with RALSpace in the UK to design and build a quantum nanosatellite similar to SpooQy-1 with the capabilities needed to beam entangled photons from space to a ground receiver. This is slated for demonstration aboard a 2022 mission. They are also collaborating with other teams to improve the ability of CubeSats to support quantum networks.</p>

<script id="gpa-inline-script-js-after">
jQuery(document).ready(function($) {
    var gatedPDFs = {"https:\/\/thequantuminsider.com\/locked\/?return_url=https%3A%2F%2Fthequantuminsider.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2025%2F07%2FTQI-2025-Q2-vUpdatedFinance-USE.pdf&post_id=2379433":2379433,"https:\/\/thequantuminsider.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2025\/04\/TQI-Quarterly-Report-Q1-2025-Official.pdf":2377535,"https:\/\/thequantuminsider.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2025\/03\/2025-02-27-Quantum-Market-Map.pdf":2376635,"https:\/\/thequantuminsider.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2025\/03\/03.03.2025-Extracted-Quantum-QKD-report-.pdf":2376316};
    var gpa_enable_cookies = true;

    $('a').each(function() {
        var linkHref = $(this).attr('href');
        if (gatedPDFs.hasOwnProperty(linkHref)) {
            $(this).on('click', function(e) {
                var pdfId = gatedPDFs[linkHref];
                if (gpa_enable_cookies && document.cookie.indexOf('pdf_access_' + pdfId + '=1') !== -1) {
                    return true;
                }
                e.preventDefault();
                if (typeof PUM !== 'undefined') {
                    PUM.open(2376276);
                }
                $('#popup-pdf-redirect').attr('data-pdf-id', pdfId).data('pdf-url', linkHref);
            });
        }
    });

    function checkHubspotResponse() {
        var found = false;
        $('.submitted-message').each(function() {
            if ($(this).text().indexOf("Thank you for filling the form, you will be redirected to a document.") !== -1) {
                found = true;
            }
        });
        if (found) {
            var pdfId = $('#popup-pdf-redirect').attr('data-pdf-id');
            var pdfUrl = $('#popup-pdf-redirect').data('pdf-url');
            if (pdfId && pdfUrl) {
                if (gpa_enable_cookies) {
                    document.cookie = "pdf_access_" + pdfId + "=1; path=/; max-age=" + (60*60*24*30);
                }
                setTimeout(function(){
                    window.location.href = pdfUrl;
                }, 2000);
            }
        }
    }

    setInterval(checkHubspotResponse, 2000);
});

<script id="tqi-profile-click-beacon">
(function () {
	"use strict";
	var CFG = {
		ajax:   "https:\/\/thequantuminsider.com\/wp-admin\/admin-ajax.php",
		nonce:  "44b18512bf",
		postId: 5200,
		title:  "SpooQy Action at a Distance — Quantum Entanglement Demonstrated Aboard Orbiting CubeSat",
		action: "tqi_profile_click"	};

	function companyFromCard(btn) {
		var n = btn.getAttribute('data-tqi-name');
		if (n) return n.trim();
		var card = btn.closest('#tqi-hovercard, .tqi-hovercard, #tqi-card') ||
		           document.getElementById('tqi-hovercard');
		if (card) {
			var nameEl = card.querySelector('#tqi-card-name, .tqi-card-name, #tqi-card-title');
			if (nameEl && nameEl.textContent) return nameEl.textContent.trim();
		}
		return btn.getAttribute('href') || '';
	}

	function send(btn) {
		var body = new URLSearchParams();
		body.set('action',  CFG.action);
		body.set('nonce',   CFG.nonce);
		body.set('post_id', CFG.postId);
		body.set('title',   CFG.title);
		body.set('company', companyFromCard(btn).slice(0, 191));
		try {
			if (navigator.sendBeacon) { navigator.sendBeacon(CFG.ajax, body); return; }
		} catch (e) {}
		try {
			fetch(CFG.ajax, { method: 'POST', body: body, keepalive: true, credentials: 'same-origin' });
		} catch (e) {}
	}

	document.addEventListener('click', function (ev) {
		var btn = ev.target && ev.target.closest
			? ev.target.closest('.tqi-card-btn-primary') : null;
		if (btn) send(btn);
	}, true);
})();
</script>
		<script id="tqi-hide-empty">
(function () {
	function hideEmpty() {
		document.querySelectorAll('.tqicontain').forEach(function (box) {
			// Did any TQI shortcode actually render content inside this container?
			var hasContent = box.querySelector('.tqi-card, .tqi-grid');

			// A .tqi-grid is only "real" if it has at least one row.
			if (hasContent && hasContent.classList.contains('tqi-grid')) {
				if (!hasContent.querySelector('.tqi-grid-item')) {
					hasContent = null;
				}
			}

			if (!hasContent) {
				box.style.display = 'none';
			}
		});
	}

	if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
		document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', hideEmpty);
	} else {
		hideEmpty();
	}
})();
</script>

          <button class="lcn-btn lcn-btn-primary" id="lcn-save" type="button">
            OK
          </button>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <style>
      :root{
        --lcn-blue:#1f5fbf;
        --lcn-text:#5b6b7a;
        --lcn-bg:#fff;
        --lcn-border:rgba(0,0,0,.08);
        --lcn-shadow: 0 12px 40px rgba(0,0,0,.18);
        --lcn-radius: 10px;
      }

      /* ===== Banner ===== */
      .lcn-banner{
        position:fixed;
        left:0; right:0; bottom:0;
        z-index:999999;
        background:var(--lcn-bg);
        border-top:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
      }
      .lcn-inner{
        max-width:1200px;
        margin:0 auto;
        padding:16px 20px max(16px, env(safe-area-inset-bottom));
        display:flex;
        align-items:center;
        justify-content:space-between;
        gap:20px;
      }
      .lcn-text{
        flex:1;
        font-size:17px;
        line-height:1.45;
        color:var(--lcn-text);
      }
      .lcn-actions{
        display:flex;
        align-items:center;
        gap:12px;
        flex-shrink:0;
      }
      .lcn-btn{
        font:inherit;
        font-size:15px;
        line-height:1.2;
        padding:8px 14px;
        border-radius:6px;
        cursor:pointer;
        text-decoration:none;
        white-space:nowrap;
        display:inline-flex;
        align-items:center;
        justify-content:center;
        border:1px solid transparent;
        background:#fff;
      }
      .lcn-btn:focus{ outline: 2px solid rgba(31,95,191,.35); outline-offset: 2px; }
      .lcn-btn-primary{
        background:var(--lcn-blue);
        border-color:var(--lcn-blue);
        color:#fff;
      }
      .lcn-btn-ghost{
        background:#fff;
        border-color:var(--lcn-blue);
        color:var(--lcn-blue);
      }

      /* ===== Modal ===== */
      .lcn-modal{
        position:fixed;
        inset:0;
        z-index:1000000;
        display:none;
      }
      .lcn-modal.is-open{ display:block; }
      .lcn-modal__backdrop{
        position:absolute;
        inset:0;
        background:rgba(0,0,0,.45);
      }
      .lcn-modal__panel{
        position:relative;
        width:min(720px, calc(100% - 32px));
        margin: clamp(16px, 6vh, 64px) auto 16px;
        background:var(--lcn-bg);
        border:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
        border-radius:var(--lcn-radius);
        box-shadow:var(--lcn-shadow);
        overflow:hidden;
      }
      .lcn-modal__header{
        padding:16px 18px;
        display:flex;
        align-items:center;
        justify-content:space-between;
        gap:10px;
        border-bottom:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
      }
      .lcn-modal__title{
        margin:0;
        font-size:18px;
        color:#1b2a3a;
      }
      .lcn-icon-btn{
        width:36px; height:36px;
        border-radius:8px;
        border:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
        background:#fff;
        cursor:pointer;
        font-size:22px;
        line-height:1;
        display:flex;
        align-items:center;
        justify-content:center;
        color:#1b2a3a;
      }
      .lcn-modal__body{
        padding:16px 18px;
      }
      .lcn-modal__desc{
        margin:0 0 14px;
        color:var(--lcn-text);
        line-height:1.5;
        font-size:15px;
      }
      .lcn-link{
        color:var(--lcn-blue);
        text-decoration:underline;
      }

      .lcn-pref{
        border:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
        border-radius:12px;
        overflow:hidden;
      }
      .lcn-pref__row{
        display:flex;
        align-items:center;
        justify-content:space-between;
        gap:16px;
        padding:14px 14px;
        border-top:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
      }
      .lcn-pref__row:first-child{ border-top:none; }
      .lcn-pref__name{
        font-weight:600;
        color:#1b2a3a;
        font-size:15px;
      }
      .lcn-pref__hint{
        margin-top:2px;
        color:var(--lcn-text);
        font-size:13px;
        line-height:1.35;
      }

      /* Switch */
      .lcn-switch{
        display:inline-flex;
        align-items:center;
        gap:10px;
        cursor:pointer;
        user-select:none;
        font-size:13px;
        color:#1b2a3a;
      }
      .lcn-switch input{
        position:absolute;
        opacity:0;
        pointer-events:none;
      }
      .lcn-switch__pill{
        width:46px;
        height:26px;
        border-radius:999px;
        border:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
        background:#eef2f6;
        position:relative;
        flex:0 0 auto;
      }
      .lcn-switch__pill::after{
        content:"";
        position:absolute;
        top:50%;
        transform:translateY(-50%);
        left:3px;
        width:20px; height:20px;
        border-radius:50%;
        background:#fff;
        border:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
        transition: left .18s ease, background .18s ease;
      }
      .lcn-switch input:checked + .lcn-switch__pill{
        background:rgba(31,95,191,.14);
        border-color:rgba(31,95,191,.35);
      }
      .lcn-switch input:checked + .lcn-switch__pill::after{
        left:22px;
        border-color:rgba(31,95,191,.35);
      }
      .lcn-switch--locked{
        cursor:default;
        opacity:.9;
      }
      .lcn-switch__pill--on{
        background:rgba(31,95,191,.14);
        border-color:rgba(31,95,191,.35);
      }
      .lcn-switch__pill--on::after{
        left:22px;
        border-color:rgba(31,95,191,.35);
      }

      .lcn-modal__footer{
        padding:14px 18px;
        display:flex;
        gap:10px;
        justify-content:flex-end;
        border-top:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
        flex-wrap:wrap;
      }

      /* ===== Mobile ===== */
      @media (max-width:768px){
        .lcn-inner{
          flex-direction:column;
          align-items:center;
          text-align:center;
          gap:12px;
          padding:14px 16px max(14px, env(safe-area-inset-bottom));
        }
        .lcn-actions{ justify-content:center; flex-wrap:wrap; }
        .lcn-modal__panel{ width: calc(100% - 24px); }
        .lcn-pref__row{ align-items:flex-start; }
      }
      @media (max-width:360px){
        .lcn-text{ font-size:16px; }
        .lcn-btn{ font-size:14px; padding:7px 10px; }
      }
    </style>

    <script>
      (function(){
        var banner = document.getElementById('lcn-banner');
        var modal  = document.getElementById('lcn-modal');
        var manageBtn = document.getElementById('lcn-manage');
        var acceptAllBtnBanner = document.getElementById('lcn-accept-all');

        var chkAnalytics = document.getElementById('lcn-analytics');
        var chkMarketing = document.getElementById('lcn-marketing');

        var btnReject = document.getElementById('lcn-reject');
        var btnAccept = document.getElementById('lcn-accept');
        var btnSave   = document.getElementById('lcn-save');

        if (!banner || !modal) return;

        var lastFocus = null;

        function secureFlag(){
          return (location.protocol === 'https:') ? '; Secure' : '';
        }

        function setConsentCookie(obj){
          try{
            var maxAge = 180 * 86400;
            var value = encodeURIComponent(JSON.stringify(obj));
            document.cookie =
              "lcn_consent=" + value +
              "; Max-Age=" + maxAge +
              "; Path=/; SameSite=Lax" + secureFlag();
          }catch(e){}
        }

        function getDefaultConsent(){
          return {
            v: 1,
            necessary: 1,
            analytics: 0,
            marketing: 0,
            ts: new Date().toISOString()
          };
        }

        function openModal(){
          lastFocus = document.activeElement;
          modal.classList.add('is-open');
          modal.setAttribute('aria-hidden','false');
          document.documentElement.style.overflow = 'hidden';
          // focus first interactive element
          setTimeout(function(){ (chkAnalytics || modal).focus && chkAnalytics.focus(); }, 0);
        }

        function closeModal(){
          modal.classList.remove('is-open');
          modal.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true');
          document.documentElement.style.overflow = '';
          if (lastFocus && lastFocus.focus) lastFocus.focus();
        }

        function saveFromToggles(){
          var consent = getDefaultConsent();
          consent.analytics = chkAnalytics && chkAnalytics.checked ? 1 : 0;
          consent.marketing = chkMarketing && chkMarketing.checked ? 1 : 0;
          setConsentCookie(consent);
          // remove UI
          closeModal();
          if (banner) banner.remove();
        }

        function rejectNonEssential(){
          if (chkAnalytics) chkAnalytics.checked = false;
          if (chkMarketing) chkMarketing.checked = false;
          saveFromToggles();
        }

        function acceptAll(){
          if (chkAnalytics) chkAnalytics.checked = true;
          if (chkMarketing) chkMarketing.checked = true;
          saveFromToggles();
        }

        // Banner buttons
        manageBtn.addEventListener('click', openModal);

        // Banner OK = Accept all (simple UX) + still offers manage cookies
        acceptAllBtnBanner.addEventListener('click', function(){
          acceptAll();
        });

        // Modal buttons
        btnReject.addEventListener('click', rejectNonEssential);
        btnAccept.addEventListener('click', acceptAll);
        btnSave.addEventListener('click', saveFromToggles);

        // Close controls (X / backdrop)
        modal.addEventListener('click', function(e){
          var t = e.target;
          if (t && t.getAttribute && t.getAttribute('data-lcn-close') === '1') {
            closeModal();
          }
        });

        // ESC to close
        document.addEventListener('keydown', function(e){
          if (!modal.classList.contains('is-open')) return;
          if (e.key === 'Escape') closeModal();
        });

        // Minimal focus trap
        document.addEventListener('keydown', function(e){
          if (!modal.classList.contains('is-open')) return;
          if (e.key !== 'Tab') return;

          var focusables = modal.querySelectorAll('button,[href],input,[tabindex]:not([tabindex="-1"])');
          if (!focusables.length) return;

          var first = focusables[0];
          var last  = focusables[focusables.length - 1];

          if (e.shiftKey && document.activeElement === first){
            e.preventDefault(); last.focus();
          } else if (!e.shiftKey && document.activeElement === last){
            e.preventDefault(); first.focus();
          }
        });
      })();
    </script>
